diagrams.title: NCC Training and Prediction Process
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing NCC operation: Training phase computes centroids from class samples; Prediction phase calculates distances to centroids and assigns nearest class
elements: New Point → Calculate Distance to Centroid 1
New Point → Calculate Distance to Centroid 2
Compare distances → Assign to Nearest Class
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the two-phase operation of NCC: efficient centroid computation during training, and simple distance-based prediction.

diagrams.title: Linear Decision Boundary in Two-Class NCC
diagrams.description: Geometric interpretation showing two class centroids with perpendicular bisector forming the decision boundary
elements: Decision boundary: line perpendicular to C1-C2 through M
Region A (left of boundary) → Class A
Region B (right of boundary) → Class B
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate that NCC produces linear decision boundaries - a key property distinguishing it from non-linear classifiers.

diagrams.title: Effect of Outliers on NCC Centroid
diagrams.description: Illustration showing how a single outlier dramatically shifts the class centroid
diagrams.purpose: Visually demonstrate why NCC is sensitive to outliers - the mean gets pulled toward extreme values.
